sn9.14:1.0.1: On one occasion a certain bhikkhu was dwelling among the Kosalans in a certain woodland thicket. Now on that occasion, when he had returned from his alms round, after his meal that bhikkhu used to descend into a pond and sniff a red lotus. Then the devata that inhabited that woodland thicket, having compassion for that bhikkhu, desiring his good, desiring to stir up a sense of urgency in him, approached him and addressed him in verse:
sn9.14:1.0.2: v.795"When you sniff this lotus flower,An item that has not been given,This is one factor of theft:You, dear sir, are a thief of scent."
sn9.14:1.0.3: v.796"I do not take, I do not damage,I sniff the lotus from afar;So for what reason do you sayThat I am a thief of scent?
sn9.14:1.0.4: v.797"One who digs up the lotus stalks,One who damages the flowers,One of such rough behaviour:Why is he not spoken to?"sn.i.205
sn9.14:1.0.5: v.798"When a person is rough and fierce,Badly soiled like a nursing cloth,I have nothing to say to him;But it's to you that I ought to speak.
sn9.14:1.0.6: v.799"For a person without blemish,Always in quest of purity,Even a mere hair's tip of evilAppears as big as a cloud."
sn9.14:1.0.7: v.800"Surely, spirit, you understand me,And you have compassion for me.Please, O spirit, speak to me again,Whenever you see such a deed."
sn9.14:1.0.8: v.801"We don’t live with your support,Nor are we your hired servant.You, bhikkhu, should know for yourselfThe way to a good destination."
sn9.14:1.0.9: Then that bhikkhu, stirred by that devata, acquired a sense of urgency.
